content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

March 7, 1996

William J. Fogarty
Orange County Central Labor Council
2740 S. Harbor Blvd., Suite C
Santa Ana, CA 92704

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-96-028

Dear Mr. Fogarty:

This is in response to your request for advice concerning
whether you may use general funds and not political funds
("campaign funds") to purchase an advertisement in the Orange
County Democratic Party's (the "Democratic Party") booklet
distributed at its yearly convention.

Your letter seeks confirmation of the advice given to you
through Ms. Lisa Cose by me on January 12, 1996, whereby I advised
that if the purchase was made from your general fund, it was not
reportable under the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

Please note that this letter is based on the facts presented
to us. The Fair Political Practices Commission does not act as
the finder of fact in providing advice. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1
FPPC Ops. 71.)

QUESTION

Is a payment for an advertisement in the Democratic Party's
booklet from the Orange County Central Labor Council (the
"Council") required to be made from campaign funds?

_________________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission Regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations,
Sections 18000-18954.

428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

CONCLUSION

No, the payment may be made from the general fund.

FACTS

The Orange County Central Labor Council (AFL-CIO) wishes to
purchase advertisement space in the Orange County Democratic
Party's Booklet at its yearly convention. This advertisement
would consist of a congratulatory message to the County Democratic
Party generally from the AFL-CIO. The FPPC has not reviewed the
actual advertisement. Ms. Lisa Cose in our telephone conversation
stated that the price paid for the advertisement would be fair
market value. The council has two separate funds from which it
pays expense, a general fund and a campaign fund. The general
fund is used for administrative and general union type purposes.
The campaign fund is used for purposes related to the council's
activities as a political organization.

ANALYSIS

The payment to the Democratic Party from the general fund
must be reported if it is a contribution or expenditure as defined
in the Act. Section 82015 and Regulation 18215 defines
contribution.

Section 82015 states:

"Contribution means a payment, a forgiveness of
a loan, a payment except to the extent that full
and adequate consideration is received unless it
is clear from the surrounding circumstances that
it is not made for political purposes. . .

Regulation 18215(a) states:

A contribution is any payment made for political
purposes for which full and adequate consideration
is not made to the donor. A payment is made for
political purposes if it is . . .

Since the council is receiving full and adequate
consideration in the form of advertisement space, the payment does
not meet the definition of contribution. (Robeck Advise Letter A-
89-489.)

The next question is whether the payment for the
advertisement is an expenditure if made from the general fund.
Regulation 18225 defines expenditure as "any monetary or
nonmonetary payment made for political purposes." Because the
council is only giving a congratulatory message to the Democratic
Party generally, the payment is not a payment for political

purposes; therefore, it is not an expenditure under Regulation
18225.2

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to call me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By:  Marte Castanos
     Intern, Legal Division

[handwritten signature]


2            This advice is limited to the Act and does not address
internal labor council rules or possible tax consequences.
